content.text: Found the issues: `ops-user` has unwanted full access on `/app`, `app-user`'s `configure` permission on `/app` is empty, and `ops-user` has no permissions at all on `/ops`. Fixing all three:

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-lifec-f9da471b-cluster-a
# Remove ops-user's incorrect access to /app
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl clear_permissions -p /app ops-user
# Fix app-user's configure permission on /app
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*"
# Grant ops-user full permissions on /ops
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /ops ops-user ".*" ".*" ".*"
# Ensure app-user has no permissions on /ops (should already be none)
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl clear_permissions -p /ops app-user 2>&1 || true
echo "=== perms /app ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /app
echo "=== perms /ops ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /ops

content.text: Permissions now correctly scoped. Let's restart the client pods and verify health.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-lifec-f9da471b-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS delete pod -l app=app-client --ignore-not-found
kubectl -n $NS delete pod -l app=ops-client --ignore-not-found
kubectl -n $NS wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=app-client --timeout=60s
kubectl -n $NS wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=ops-client --timeout=60s
